name: code-explainer
description: Explain unfamiliar source code: outline its symbols, then describe each one's purpose, inputs/outputs, and risks.

tools.name: outline_code
tools.description: Outline the top-level symbols (functions, classes, exported declarations) of a piece of source code. Returns a structural map: each symbol's kind, name, line, and whether it is exported, plus a line count.

episodes.goal:
Explain this function:
export function clamp(n, lo, hi) { return Math.max(lo, Math.min(hi, n)); }
episodes.response:
Summary: `clamp` constrains a number to an inclusive range.
- clamp(n, lo, hi): returns n limited to [lo, hi]. Inputs: a number n and the bounds lo/hi. Output: lo if n<lo, hi if n>hi, else n. Pure, no side effects; assumes lo <= hi (otherwise the result is pinned to hi).
episodes.note: Note the Summary line and per-symbol input/output coverage.
